outbound_official.notes: Taiwan's Bureau of Consular Affairs (BOCA), Ministry of Foreign Affairs, publishes the official outbound visa-free list at https://www.boca.gov.tw/cp-149-4486-7785a-2.html. As of May 12, 2025, 173 countries and territories in total accord visa-free entry, landing visas, or e-visas to ROC (Taiwan) ordinary passport holders (per taiwan.gov.tw). The BOCA page explicitly lists visa-free destinations only; BOCA does not publish a consolidated single-page list of outbound VOA or e-visa destinations for Taiwan nationals — those must be verified country by country via destination-side sources. Time-limited visa-free schemes: Thailand, Brunei, Philippines valid until July 31, 2026; North Macedonia until March 31, 2030. Stay limits vary: most 90-day; Belize, Dominican Republic, Malaysia, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Singapore 30-day; Oman 14-day; Brunei, Philippines, Thailand 14-day (trial). Honduras and Dominican Republic for ordinary passports only. Certain passport holders with specific listed birthplaces are ineligible per BOCA notice.

visa_free.notes: Ordinary passport holders and Certificate of Identity holders; scheme valid until July 31, 2026; proof of accommodation and sufficient funds required
Ordinary passport holders; extendable up to 180 days
Ordinary passport holders only; diplomatic/official passport holders excluded from this scheme
Ordinary passport holders only; diplomatic/official passport holders excluded from this scheme
Ordinary passport holders; scheme valid until March 31, 2030
Ordinary passport holders only; scheme valid until July 31, 2026; proof of accommodation and sufficient funds required
Ordinary passport holders only; scheme valid until July 31, 2026; proof of accommodation and sufficient funds required
Ordinary passport holders; extendable up to 180 days

visa_on_arrival.nationality: Holders of emergency or temporary passports from visa-exempt countries (except USA)
visa_on_arrival.notes: Eligible for landing visa at Taiwan Taoyuan International Airport Bureau of Consular Affairs office. At other airports (Taipei Songshan, Taichung, Kaohsiung), travelers must first obtain a temporary entry permit before applying for a visa at designated MOFA offices. Fee: NT$1,600 (or free for reciprocal agreement countries) + NT$800 handling fee. Not extendable. Stay begins day after arrival.

e_visa.notes: Eligible for eVisa (also has 14-day visa-free access)
Ordinary and special passport holders only; eligibility adjusted per bilateral agreements

e_visa.nationality: Southeast Asian group tourists (Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Vietnam)
e_visa.notes: Must travel with designated travel agencies under the Tourism Administration; group tour requirement; scheme extended through December 31, 2026

e_visa.nationality: Business visitors from Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Iran, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka
e_visa.notes: Must be recommended by Taiwan External Trade Development Council (TAITRA) overseas offices

visa_policy.notes: Taiwan's visa policy is administered by the Bureau of Consular Affairs (BOCA), Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Passport validity of at least 6 months is required for all entries. Time-limited visa-free schemes: Thailand, Brunei, Philippines valid until July 31, 2026 (extended per BOCA announcement https://www.boca.gov.tw/cp-220-7850-9cbb1-2.html); North Macedonia until March 31, 2030. Southeast Asian group tourist eVisa scheme extended through December 31, 2026. eVisa max stay days not specified on official page — consult BOCA for current details. The official eVisa application portal is https://visawebapp.boca.gov.tw/BOCA_EVISA/

credential.subtype: any valid visa (electronic visas must be printed)

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid entry visa issued by the United States (electronic visas must be printed) may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC) — multiple-entry, valid 90 days, up to 30 days per stay. Passport must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Must possess onward/return air or ferry ticket. Must never have been employed as a blue-collar worker in Taiwan.

credential.subtype: valid resident or permanent resident card

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid US resident or permanent resident card may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC) — multiple-entry, valid 90 days, up to 30 days per stay. Passport must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Must possess onward/return air or ferry ticket. Must never have been employed as a blue-collar worker in Taiwan.

credential.subtype: permanent resident card expired within 10 years of date of expected arrival

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a US permanent resident card expired within 10 years of the date of expected arrival in Taiwan may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC). Passport must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Must possess onward/return air or ferry ticket. Must never have been employed as a blue-collar worker in Taiwan.

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid or recently expired (within 10 years) Canadian resident or permanent resident card may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC).

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen visa

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid entry visa issued by a Schengen Agreement signatory country may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC) — multiple-entry, valid 90 days, up to 30 days per stay. Passport must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Must possess onward/return air or ferry ticket. Must never have been employed as a blue-collar worker in Taiwan.

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen visa or residence certificate

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Armenia, Colombia, Kazakhstan, and Uzbekistan holding a valid visa or resident certificate issued by a Schengen country are eligible for Taiwan eVisa. Armenia, Kazakhstan, and Uzbekistan eligibility is valid through March 31, 2027.

credential.subtype: valid US resident certificate or visa valid for more than 180 days

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Armenia, Colombia, Kazakhstan, and Uzbekistan holding a valid US resident certificate or visa valid for more than 180 days are eligible for Taiwan eVisa. Armenia, Kazakhstan, and Uzbekistan eligibility is valid through March 31, 2027.

credential.subtype: prior ROC visitor visa or alien resident certificate issued within the past 10 years (excluding FL/X category visas and foreign laborer ARCs)

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam who previously held a ROC (Taiwan) entry visa or alien resident certificate issued within the past 10 years, with no immigration violations or law-breaking history in Taiwan. Excluded: visas marked 'FL' (foreign laborer) or 'X' (others), and resident cards indicating foreign laborer status. Certificate valid for 90 days, multiple-entry, up to 30 days per stay.

cbi.notes: Taiwan has no formal Citizenship-by-Investment (CBI) program. Citizenship is obtainable through naturalization after 5 years of consecutive legal residency, subject to language proficiency and income requirements. Taiwan generally requires renunciation of prior citizenship unless the applicant holds a reserved occupational qualification. No official government source advertises a CBI product. Source checked: immigration.gov.tw, en.mofa.gov.tw.

programs.name: Resident Visa for Investment
programs.notes: Minimum USD 200,000 investment required as stipulated in 'Regulations on the Operation of Foreign Investors or Representatives of Foreign Corporations Applying for Resident Visas'. Proof of investment issued by competent central government authority required. Processing time: 8 working days for domestic applications.

programs.name: Alien Permanent Resident Certificate (Plum Blossom Card) - Business Investment
programs.notes: Investment of NTD 15 million in a profitable ROC business, creating employment for 5 or more ROC nationals, maintained for 3 consecutive years. Not subject to 183-day/year residency requirement. Application fee: NTD 10,000. Dual ROC citizenship not permitted.

programs.name: Alien Permanent Resident Certificate (Plum Blossom Card) - Government Bonds
programs.notes: Purchase of ROC central government bonds with face value exceeding NTD 30 million, held for 3 consecutive years. Not subject to 183-day/year residency requirement. Application fee: NTD 10,000.

programs.name: Entrepreneur Visa (Startup/Business Founder Residence)
programs.notes: Initial 2-year validity, extendable in 2-year increments. For renewal: annual gross revenue of at least NTD 3 million OR average over 3 years, OR operating expenses at least NTD 1 million/year, OR employment of 3+ full-time ROC nationals. Processing: 30 working days initial, 22 working days for extension. Apply via Foreign Professionals Online Application Platform.

programs.name: Employment Gold Card
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals with demonstrated professional expertise in one of 12 designated fields: Architecture, Biotechnology, Culture and Arts, Digital, Economy, Education, Environment, Finance, Law, National Defense, Science & Technology, Sports. Applicants must meet field-specific salary thresholds or hold recognized qualifications.
programs.processing_time: Joint review by National Immigration Agency, Ministry of Labor, Ministry of Foreign Affairs, and specialized authorities
programs.notes: Combined open work permit, resident visa, Alien Resident Certificate, and re-entry permit valid up to 3 years. Open work authorization (work for any employer). Special tax incentives for high earners. Apply via Foreign Professionals Online Application Platform at https://coa.immigration.gov.tw/coa-frontend/four-in-one/entry/golden-card. Official site also at foreigntalentact.ndc.gov.tw.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Visitor Visa
programs.eligibility: Nationals of Taiwan visa-exempt countries only. Age 30+: annual salary at least USD 40,000 (within last 2 years); Age 20-29: annual salary at least USD 20,000. Alternative: hold a digital nomad visa from another country. Bank statements must show average at least USD 10,000/month over 6 months. Must hold proof of international health insurance.
programs.processing_time: Applications submitted to ROC overseas mission, forwarded to National Development Council for review; or domestic applications at BOCA/MOFA offices at least 10 working days before stay expires
programs.notes: Program launched per Act for the Recruitment and Employment of Foreign Professionals. Maximum stay duration and renewal period not specified verbatim on BOCA's official page. Also available via: https://foreigntalentact.ndc.gov.tw and https://digitalnomad.ndc.gov.tw/. Program information also at https://talent.nat.gov.tw/visa/digital-nomad

validation_notes: CORRECTED: Dominican Republic max_stay_days was erroneously listed under the 90-day visa_free group in the prior record. Official BOCA page confirms Dominican Republic ordinary passport holders receive 30 days, not 90. Record now correctly shows 30 days.

CORRECTED: Brunei notes updated to include 'Certificate of Identity holders' as eligible, per live BOCA page language ('ordinary passports and Certificate of Identity holders'). Prior record stated 'ordinary passports' only.

CORRECTED: Landing visa (visa_on_arrival) notes updated to clarify airport-specific procedure: Taoyuan Airport has on-site BOCA office; other airports (Songshan, Taichung, Kaohsiung) require temporary entry permit first, then visa at MOFA offices.
